Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed verbal clause with directional particle
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To become confused or lose one's sense of direction in life
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, family advice, youth conversations
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Associated with mountain travel and losing paths in the highlands
First Example Sentence: Ma b'aj tu'n twi' qa naq ma txi'j te jun xnaq'tzb'il.
Second Example Sentence: Atz ma b'aj tu'n twi' te kyjaq'lal q'ij.
Third Example Sentence: Qa mam kub' tq'ma nqe ma b'aj tu'n twi' te qtx'otx'.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone who speaks beautifully but acts dishonestly
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used in oral political commentary in western Mam regions
First Example Sentence: Kyaq b'ix toj q'ij te winq, per mib'a'n tu'n.
Second Example Sentence: Ma kub' qnima' te kyaq b'ix toj q'ij.
Third Example Sentence: Aj il te kyaq b'ix toj q'ij kyxol xjal.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To bring hope or positive change
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to sunrise symbolism in Mam oral tradition
First Example Sentence: Te alaxnaq tzaj tqanil q'iij kyib'aj xjal.
Second Example Sentence: Qa b'ixan tzaj tqanil q'iij toj tnam.
Third Example Sentence: Tzaj tqanil q'iij qa ma kub' tq'o'n jun tb'een.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To interfere in matters that are not one's business
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional reference to entering another household without invitation
First Example Sentence: Mina okx tqan tja toj kyajb'il junx.
Second Example Sentence: Atz b'ix okx tqan tja te qman.
Third Example Sentence: Okx tqan tja qa ma txi' tqani'n jun tnam.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Compares a person to standing above the sun
First Example Sentence: Jaw naq twitz q'ij te alkyee'.
Second Example Sentence: Mi nra tqanb'il jaw naq twitz q'ij.
Third Example Sentence: Qa kub' b'aj tq'ijil, jaw naq twitz q'ij.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Knowledge that deeply transforms a person
First Example Sentence: Tz'el t-xnaq'tzb'il toj qanima te talnaq.
Second Example Sentence: Mib'a'n aq'njil wi' qa mi tz'el t-xnaq'tzb'il toj qanima.
Third Example Sentence: Qa ma tz'el t-xnaq'tzb'il toj qanima, ma kub' tb'antik.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To create peace and warmth in the home
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Connected to fire and sunlight imagery in Mam homes

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Refers to speech disappearing into the forest
